explanation: This LTL property captures the requirement that whenever a failure (apfail) occurs, the autopilot will eventually transition from the STANDBY state (state_is_STANDBY) to the MANEUVER state (state_is_MANEUVER).  It uses the "until" operator (U) to express the condition that the system remains in STANDBY until the MANEUVER state is reached after apfail.

This LTL specification asserts that whenever a failure (apfail) occurs, the system will eventually transition from the STANDBY state (state_is_STANDBY) to the MANEUVER state (state_is_MANEUVER).  It uses the "until" operator (U) to express the condition that the system must remain in the STANDBY state until the MANEUVER state is reached after the failure. The use of G ensures that this property holds globally, i.e., at all times.

It uses the "until" operator (U) to express the condition that the autopilot remains in STANDBY until the MANEUVER state is reached after the failure. The use of the next operator (X) allows us to check for a change of state after the failure occurs.

It uses the "until" operator (U) to express the condition that the transition must happen eventually after the failure, ensuring that the system responds appropriately to the failure condition.  It also addresses the potential for the system to remain in a transition state for a period of time.

It uses the atomic propositions state_is_STANDBY and state_is_MANEUVER to track the autopilot's state. The formula ensures that if a failure occurs (apfail is true), then eventually the autopilot will be in the MANEUVER state (state_is_MANEUVER). The use of the implication (->) ensures that the requirement is only relevant when a failure occurs. If there is no failure, the formula does not constrain the system's behavior.

It uses the atomic propositions state_is_STANDBY and state_is_MANEUVER to represent the autopilot's states and apfail to represent the failure condition. The formula G (state_is_STANDBY & apfail -> F state_is_MANEUVER) states that globally (G), if the autopilot is in STANDBY (state_is_STANDBY) and a failure occurs (apfail), then eventually (F) the autopilot will be in the MANEUVER state (state_is_MANEUVER).
